heading: Traralgon vs Traralgon South — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Traralgon, Victoria and Traralgon South, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Traralgon South has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Traralgon (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Traralgon or Traralgon South safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Traralgon has a flood risk score of 40/100, while Traralgon South scores 37/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Traralgon scores 36/100 for bushfire risk. Traralgon South scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Traralgon or Traralgon South?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Traralgon South has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.92% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
